# GAP_ANALYSIS.md
# Generated by Codex during bootstrap from PROJECT(3).md because PROJECT.md is currently absent.
# Do not edit manually except to resolve planning metadata or status fields as gaps are closed.

## GAP-001 — Canonicalize project docs
phase: 0
status: closed
type: docs
blocked_by: none
goal: Make the repository documentation match the LaCucina mobile app brief and remove leftover game-template wording.
scope: README.md, PROJECT.md, PROJECT(3).md, docs/ if needed.
acceptance_criteria: A canonical PROJECT.md exists with the LaCucina brief; README.md no longer says template-game-engine; PROJECT(3).md is removed or clearly superseded; no application source code is created.
tests: Manual documentation review; rg checks for legacy template-game-engine/game-engine wording.
closes_when: The repository has one clear project brief at PROJECT.md and the README describes LaCucina as a smartphone app project.
escalate_if: The current PROJECT(3).md content is not the intended source of truth.
do_not: Do not scaffold the app, choose a framework, or change product scope in this gap.

## GAP-002 — Resolve MVP direction and mobile stack
phase: 0
status: closed
type: product
blocked_by: DL-001, DL-002, DL-014
goal: Lock the first-build product direction and chosen mobile framework before any scaffold work.
scope: PROJECT.md, DECISIONS.md, docs/architecture.md if needed.
acceptance_criteria: The MVP direction is documented as personal cookbook, creator-first, or open platform; the target platforms and framework are documented; any rejected options are listed as out of scope for MVP.
tests: Manual review against DECISIONS.md; no unresolved DL-001 or DL-002 remains.
closes_when: The framework and MVP direction are resolved and reflected in project documentation.
escalate_if: The selected direction requires marketplace, monetization, public publishing, or credentials in the first build.
do_not: Do not scaffold or install dependencies in this gap.

## GAP-003 — Lock MVP functional scope
phase: 0
status: closed
type: product
blocked_by: GAP-002, DL-005, DL-006, DL-007, DL-008, DL-009, DL-010, DL-011, DL-012
goal: Turn the LaCucina brief into a small MVP contract for recipes, cookbook organization, portion scaling, and meal planning.
scope: PROJECT.md, GAP_ANALYSIS.md follow-up refinement, docs/mvp-scope.md if needed.
acceptance_criteria: MVP recipe fields are listed; cookbook/category scope is listed; first planner type is selected; sharing, publishing, nutrition, media, and Freak Mode are explicitly in or out of MVP.
tests: Manual review against PROJECT.md; every decision listed in blocked_by is resolved.
closes_when: A developer can implement MVP features without guessing product behavior.
escalate_if: A requested MVP item expands into public marketplace, payments, social feed, or advanced nutrition.
do_not: Do not add screens, domain models, or persistence code in this gap.

## GAP-004 — Define account, storage, and privacy boundaries
phase: 0
status: closed
type: product
blocked_by: GAP-002, DL-003, DL-004
goal: Define how MVP data is owned, stored, recovered, and protected.
scope: PROJECT.md, docs/privacy.md, docs/data-strategy.md if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Account/auth stance is documented; storage/backend/offline stance is documented; recipe privacy defaults and deletion expectations are documented; sensitive data boundaries are explicit.
tests: Manual review; no unresolved DL-003 or DL-004 remains.
closes_when: Data and privacy requirements are clear enough to design repositories and tests.
escalate_if: The MVP requires external credentials, private APIs, paid services, or collection of sensitive personal data without privacy requirements.
do_not: Do not implement auth, database, sync, analytics, or tracking.

## GAP-005 — Define architecture and folder contract
phase: 0
status: closed
type: foundation
blocked_by: GAP-003, GAP-004
goal: Document the selected framework architecture while preserving domain, application, data, and presentation separation.
scope: docs/architecture.md, PROJECT.md, README.md if needed.
acceptance_criteria: App, core, features, domain, application, data, presentation, assets, and tests folders are mapped to the selected framework; import boundaries are documented; state management and dependency composition approach are named.
tests: Manual architecture review; future scaffold gap can follow the documented contract without interpretation.
closes_when: The architecture note gives enough structure to scaffold the project consistently.
escalate_if: The architecture would contradict a resolved framework or require changing closed gap contracts.
do_not: Do not create production source folders beyond documentation examples.

## GAP-006 — Define future publishing and media guardrails
phase: 0
status: closed
type: product
blocked_by: DL-009, DL-012, DL-013
goal: Keep creator, publishing, ownership, moderation, and media ambitions from leaking into the private cookbook MVP.
scope: PROJECT.md, docs/future-platform-guardrails.md if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Publishing is either excluded from MVP or has a narrow MVP boundary; media timing is decided; ownership/copyright and moderation prerequisites are documented for future public content.
tests: Manual review against out-of-scope list.
closes_when: Future platform features are documented as later phases unless explicitly selected for MVP.
escalate_if: Public publishing, creator profiles, subscriptions, marketplace, or moderation are required immediately.
do_not: Do not implement public profiles, feeds, subscriptions, marketplace, media upload, or moderation tools.

## GAP-007 — Scaffold selected web-first project
phase: 1
status: closed
type: foundation
blocked_by: GAP-005
goal: Initialize the chosen React + Vite + TypeScript web-first framework with the documented folder contract.
scope: Framework root files, src/app, src/core, src/features, src/assets, test bootstrap.
acceptance_criteria: The app builds in React + Vite + TypeScript; source folders match the architecture contract; placeholder assets are minimal and marked; no unrelated product features are implemented.
tests: Framework build or doctor command; minimal generated test command if available.
closes_when: A clean scaffold can be built and tested locally.
escalate_if: The selected framework tooling requires network access, paid tooling, signing credentials, or platform accounts that are unavailable.
do_not: Do not implement recipe, planner, auth, or persistence behavior.

## GAP-008 — Add lint, format, tests, and CI baseline
phase: 1
status: closed
type: quality
blocked_by: GAP-007
goal: Establish repeatable local quality checks before feature work starts.
scope: Lint config, formatter config, unit/widget test config, CI workflow if appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: A single documented command runs lint/format checks; a single documented command runs tests; CI or a documented local substitute runs the same checks.
tests: Run lint/format command and test command.
closes_when: Quality commands pass on the scaffold.
escalate_if: CI provider, package registry, or dependency installation requires credentials or network approval.
do_not: Do not add broad dependencies unrelated to the selected framework quality baseline.

## GAP-009 — Create app shell, routing, theme, and config
phase: 1
status: closed
type: foundation
blocked_by: GAP-008
goal: Add the minimal app shell needed for future screens without feature logic.
scope: App entry, navigation/routing shell, theme/design tokens, environment/config placeholders.
acceptance_criteria: App launches to a neutral shell screen; safe areas are respected; theme tokens exist; configuration has non-secret placeholders only.
tests: Component/widget smoke test for shell; launch test if supported.
closes_when: The shell is visible in tests and contains no direct domain, network, or persistence logic.
escalate_if: Routing or state management choices conflict with the architecture contract.
do_not: Do not add recipe UI, planner UI, auth flow, analytics, or secrets.

## GAP-010 — Add dependency composition root
phase: 1
status: closed
type: foundation
blocked_by: GAP-009
goal: Provide a framework-native way to wire use cases, repositories, and presentation controllers.
scope: App-level providers, service locator, dependency injection module, test overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Dependencies can be swapped for fakes in tests; UI code depends on interfaces or use cases rather than concrete data adapters; no real external service is wired.
tests: Unit/component test showing a fake dependency override.
closes_when: Future features can register use cases and repositories without direct UI construction.
escalate_if: The chosen state management or DI approach is unclear or incompatible with tests.
do_not: Do not implement real repositories, auth clients, databases, or network calls.

## GAP-011 — Add launch smoke test
phase: 1
status: closed
type: quality
blocked_by: GAP-010
goal: Prove the scaffolded app starts reliably on the selected test runner.
scope: Smoke test files, test fixtures if required.
acceptance_criteria: The smoke test launches the app shell and verifies an accessible root element; test is deterministic and does not need network or a real device account.
tests: Run the smoke test command and broader test command if available.
closes_when: The smoke test passes in the local environment.
escalate_if: The framework requires a simulator/device that is not available and no headless test fallback exists.
do_not: Do not cover feature flows in this gap.

## GAP-012 — Model recipes and ingredients
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-011, GAP-003
goal: Define pure domain models for recipes, ingredients, steps, and basic validation.
scope: Recipe feature domain layer and unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Recipe has ID, title, base servings, ingredients, steps, category/cookbook reference as selected, and MVP metadata; invalid empty titles, invalid serving counts, and invalid ingredient quantities are rejected with explicit errors.
tests: Unit tests for valid model creation and validation failures.
closes_when: Recipe domain objects are tested without UI, database, network, or platform APIs.
escalate_if: Required recipe fields are still unresolved or nutrition/media fields become mandatory.
do_not: Do not implement UI, persistence, import/export, or public visibility behavior.

## GAP-013 — Implement portion scaling rules
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-012
goal: Add deterministic portion scaling for ingredient quantities.
scope: Recipe domain services/value objects and unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Scaled quantity equals original quantity multiplied by target servings divided by base servings; base recipe values remain unchanged; invalid target servings fail with explicit errors; rounding/formatting rules are documented.
tests: Unit tests for 2 to 6 serving example, fractional quantities, unchanged base values, and invalid inputs.
closes_when: Portion scaling passes tests using pure domain logic.
escalate_if: Unit conversion, nutrition, or non-linear ingredient scaling is required for MVP.
do_not: Do not add UI sliders, persistence, or external nutrition services.

## GAP-014 — Model cookbooks and categories
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-012, DL-006
goal: Define cookbook/category structures for organizing recipes.
scope: Cookbook feature domain layer and unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: The selected cookbook/category model supports creating, renaming, ordering if required, and assigning recipes; duplicate/empty names are handled predictably.
tests: Unit tests for category creation, recipe assignment, validation, and ordering if selected.
closes_when: Recipes can be organized by the resolved structure without UI assumptions.
escalate_if: Nested tabs, multi-cookbook ownership, or public cookbook behavior becomes mandatory.
do_not: Do not implement drag-and-drop UI, public sharing, or remote sync.

## GAP-015 — Model meal planning
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-013, DL-007
goal: Define the first meal-planning model using saved recipes as building blocks.
scope: Meal planning feature domain layer and unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: The selected planning type supports adding a recipe entry, choosing servings per entry, moving/removing entries, and identifying empty plan slots or days.
tests: Unit tests for creating a plan, adding recipe references, serving overrides, removal, and empty-state behavior.
closes_when: The first planner type is represented as pure domain logic.
escalate_if: Monthly planning, custom recurring loops, calorie/macro targets, or sync requirements are added to MVP.
do_not: Do not build calendar UI, notifications, or nutrition calculations.

## GAP-016 — Add recipe application use cases
phase: 2
status: closed
type: application
blocked_by: GAP-013, GAP-014
goal: Create testable use cases for recipe CRUD, search/filter, favorites if selected, and portion preview.
scope: Recipe feature application layer and unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Use cases cover create, update, delete, get details, list/search/filter, and scaled portion preview; errors are explicit; tests use fake repositories.
tests: Unit tests with repository fakes for success, validation failure, not found, and empty results.
closes_when: Recipe behavior can be exercised without UI or real persistence.
escalate_if: Sharing, publishing, media upload, or auth ownership is required inside recipe use cases.
do_not: Do not call network, database, or platform APIs directly.

## GAP-017 — Add cookbook application use cases
phase: 2
status: closed
type: application
blocked_by: GAP-014, GAP-016
goal: Create use cases for managing cookbook/category organization.
scope: Cookbook feature application layer and unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Use cases cover list categories/cookbooks, create/rename/delete selected structures, assign/unassign recipes, and return empty/error states.
tests: Unit tests with fakes for category lifecycle, recipe assignment, duplicate names, and not found cases.
closes_when: Organization behavior is testable without UI or persistence details.
escalate_if: Multi-user public cookbook ownership is required.
do_not: Do not implement visual management screens or remote APIs.

## GAP-018 — Add meal planner application use cases
phase: 2
status: closed
type: application
blocked_by: GAP-015, GAP-016
goal: Create use cases for building and editing the first meal plan format.
scope: Meal planning feature application layer and unit tests.
acceptance_criteria: Use cases cover loading a plan, adding saved recipes, changing servings, moving/removing entries, and returning empty/error states.
tests: Unit tests with fakes for plan lifecycle, missing recipe references, serving changes, and empty plan.
closes_when: Meal planning can be tested without UI or real persistence.
escalate_if: Recurring sync, notifications, or nutrition targets become required.
do_not: Do not build planner UI or platform calendar integration.

## GAP-019 — Define repository interfaces and mappers
phase: 2
status: closed
type: data
blocked_by: GAP-016, GAP-017, GAP-018, GAP-004
goal: Define persistence boundaries for recipes, cookbook structures, and plans.
scope: Feature data contracts, DTOs/mappers if needed, fake/in-memory implementations for tests.
acceptance_criteria: Repository interfaces expose required app use cases; DTO/mapping rules are documented if persistence is selected; tests prove fakes satisfy contracts.
tests: Unit tests for repository contract fakes and mapper round trips if DTOs exist.
closes_when: Data contracts are ready for local storage or backend adapters.
escalate_if: Storage/auth/backend decisions are unresolved or require credentials.
do_not: Do not implement real local database, network client, or sync in this gap.

## GAP-020 — Build navigation graph and route guards
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-019, GAP-010
goal: Add app navigation for the selected MVP screens with mobile-safe routing behavior.
scope: App routing/navigation files, route tests, minimal placeholder screens.
acceptance_criteria: Routes exist for recipe list, recipe detail, recipe form, cookbook/category management, and planner if selected; route guards reflect auth/account decision; back navigation behaves predictably.
tests: Component/widget navigation tests.
closes_when: Placeholder routes can be navigated in tests without feature UI logic.
escalate_if: Auth or onboarding flow is required but unresolved.
do_not: Do not implement full screen content in this gap.

## GAP-021 — Build recipe list, search, and filter screen
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-020, GAP-016, GAP-017
goal: Let users find saved recipes by title and selected organization metadata.
scope: Recipe list presentation screen, view model/controller, component tests.
acceptance_criteria: Screen handles loading, error, empty, offline/unavailable if relevant, populated list, search/filter, and accessible tap targets on small phones.
tests: Component/widget tests for each UI state, search/filter behavior, and accessibility labels.
closes_when: Recipe list screen passes tests against fake use cases.
escalate_if: Search requires remote indexing or public catalog behavior.
do_not: Do not implement public marketplace, social feed, or real persistence.

## GAP-022 — Build recipe create/edit/delete flow
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-020, GAP-016
goal: Allow users to add, edit, and delete private recipes using the resolved MVP fields.
scope: Recipe form presentation, validation state, delete confirmation, component tests.
acceptance_criteria: Form supports required MVP fields; validation errors are visible and accessible; keyboard/safe-area behavior works on small phones; delete requires confirmation; loading/error states are represented.
tests: Component/widget tests for valid submit, validation errors, edit prefill, delete confirmation, and failure states.
closes_when: Recipe form flow passes tests using fake use cases.
escalate_if: Media upload, account ownership, or publishing settings become mandatory in the form.
do_not: Do not store data directly from UI or bypass application use cases.

## GAP-023 — Build recipe detail and cooking portion screen
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-020, GAP-013, GAP-016
goal: Show recipe details and make portion recalculation clear during cooking.
scope: Recipe detail presentation, portion control, scaled ingredient display, component tests.
acceptance_criteria: Screen shows title, metadata, ingredients, steps, notes if selected, base servings, target servings control, and scaled quantities; base values remain distinguishable; loading/error/empty states exist.
tests: Component/widget tests for scaling display, invalid serving input, loading, error, and small-screen layout.
closes_when: Users can preview scaled ingredients in tested UI without mutating the base recipe.
escalate_if: Advanced unit conversion, nutrition, or cooking timers are required.
do_not: Do not implement timers, notifications, media playback, or nutrition calculations.

## GAP-024 — Build cookbook/category management UI
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-020, GAP-017
goal: Let users organize recipes using the selected cookbook/category model.
scope: Cookbook/category presentation screens or sheets, view models/controllers, component tests.
acceptance_criteria: UI supports selected create/rename/delete/assign actions; empty/error/loading states exist; destructive actions are confirmed; small phone and accessibility requirements are met.
tests: Component/widget tests for organization lifecycle and failure states.
closes_when: Organization UI passes tests against fake use cases.
escalate_if: Public cookbook sharing, collaboration, or nested structures are required.
do_not: Do not implement public profiles, subscriptions, or marketplace behavior.

## GAP-025 — Build first meal planner UI
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-020, GAP-018
goal: Let users build the resolved first meal plan from saved recipes.
scope: Planner presentation screen, recipe picker, serving override controls, component tests.
acceptance_criteria: UI supports the selected weekly/template/training plan; users can add saved recipes, set servings, remove entries, and see empty/error/loading/offline states when relevant.
tests: Component/widget tests for adding recipe entries, changing servings, removing entries, empty plan, and missing recipe failures.
closes_when: The first planner flow passes tests with fake use cases.
escalate_if: Monthly planning, calendar integration, notifications, or nutrition targets become required.
do_not: Do not implement platform calendar APIs, reminders, push notifications, or macro tracking.

## GAP-026 — Extract reusable mobile UI components
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-021, GAP-022, GAP-023, GAP-024, GAP-025
goal: Consolidate repeated UI primitives without creating speculative abstractions.
scope: Shared presentation components used by at least two screens, component tests.
acceptance_criteria: Reused controls for loading/error/empty states, form fields, portion inputs, recipe cards, and destructive confirmations exist only where duplicated; accessibility labels are included.
tests: Component/widget tests for shared components and one integration check per consuming screen if needed.
closes_when: Screens share tested components without changing behavior.
escalate_if: The extraction would require broad rewrites or alter closed screen contracts.
do_not: Do not build a design system beyond current repeated needs.

## GAP-027 — Complete cross-screen UI state pass
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-026
goal: Verify every implemented screen handles loading, error, empty, unavailable/offline, and small-screen states consistently.
scope: All implemented presentation screens and component tests.
acceptance_criteria: Each screen has tested states; touch targets and labels are accessible; keyboard behavior does not obscure forms; safe areas are respected.
tests: Component/widget tests for missing states plus manual small-phone review if available.
closes_when: No MVP screen lacks required state handling.
escalate_if: Offline/unavailable behavior depends on unresolved storage/backend decisions.
do_not: Do not add new product features while doing the state pass.

## GAP-028 — Implement local persistence adapter
phase: 4
status: closed
type: data
blocked_by: GAP-019, DL-004
goal: Persist private recipes, cookbook organization, and meal plans according to the resolved storage strategy.
scope: Data adapters, local database/filesystem config, migrations if needed, data tests.
acceptance_criteria: Data survives app restart in the selected local strategy; repository contracts pass against the real adapter; migration/versioning baseline exists if the storage choice needs it; deletion behavior is tested.
tests: Data/integration tests for create/read/update/delete, mapper round trips, persistence across adapter restart, and error handling.
closes_when: MVP data persists locally without real external services.
escalate_if: Cloud sync, multi-device accounts, encryption requirements, or external backend credentials become mandatory.
do_not: Do not add analytics, tracking, public API clients, or real user data fixtures.

## GAP-029 — Implement optional share/export flow
phase: 4
status: closed
type: integration
blocked_by: GAP-023, DL-008
goal: Add the resolved MVP sharing/export mechanism if it is in scope.
scope: Share/export application contract, platform share adapter or text export, UI entry point, tests.
acceptance_criteria: Sharing/export follows the resolved scope; private data boundaries are respected; failures are shown to the user; no public publishing is implied unless explicitly resolved.
tests: Unit tests for export formatting; component/widget tests for share entry and failure states; platform adapter test/fake where supported.
closes_when: The selected share/export behavior works through a tested interface.
escalate_if: Private links, public URLs, backend hosting, copyright policy, or moderation are required.
do_not: Do not build social feed, public profiles, marketplace, or subscriptions.

## GAP-030 — Implement auth or backend sync only if selected
phase: 4
status: dropped
type: integration
blocked_by: GAP-019, DL-003, DL-004
goal: Add account/auth/backend integration only if the resolved MVP requires it.
scope: Auth/session contracts, backend client, secure config placeholders, integration tests with fakes.
acceptance_criteria: If selected, auth/session and sync behavior are behind interfaces, tested with fakes, and keep secrets out of the repo; if not selected, this gap is dropped with rationale.
tests: Unit/integration tests using fake auth/backend clients; config checks proving no secrets are committed.
closes_when: Selected account/backend behavior is tested or the gap is formally dropped as out of MVP.
escalate_if: Real credentials, paid services, private APIs, or app-store accounts are required.
do_not: Do not hard-code secrets, call real services in tests, or add tracking.

## GAP-031 — Add critical flow integration coverage
phase: 5
status: closed
type: quality
blocked_by: GAP-028, GAP-025
goal: Cover the MVP success loop end to end with deterministic tests.
scope: Integration/E2E tests, fixtures/fakes, test runner config.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover create recipe, find recipe, scale servings, cook from detail, add recipe to planner, and reopen persisted data; tests do not rely on real external services.
tests: Run integration/E2E command plus unit/component test suite.
closes_when: The MVP success loop passes integration coverage locally.
escalate_if: Device/simulator access is required and no supported local runner is available.
do_not: Do not broaden into marketplace, publishing, or payment scenarios.

## GAP-032 — Perform accessibility QA pass
phase: 5
status: closed
type: quality
blocked_by: GAP-027, GAP-031
goal: Make the MVP usable on small phones with accessible labels, focus order, and readable controls.
scope: All MVP screens, accessibility tests/checklists, documentation of known limits.
acceptance_criteria: Interactive elements have labels; focus/order is reasonable; touch targets are readable; forms remain usable with keyboard; color contrast and text sizing pass selected framework checks where available.
tests: Accessibility checks supported by the framework plus manual small-phone review.
closes_when: Accessibility issues found in the pass are fixed or documented with explicit follow-up gaps.
escalate_if: Requirements exceed MVP scope or require platform-specific audits not available locally.
do_not: Do not redesign the product or add new features during QA.

## GAP-033 — Perform performance and resilience pass
phase: 5
status: closed
type: quality
blocked_by: GAP-031
goal: Check startup, navigation, recipe lists, planner interactions, and persistence error handling.
scope: Performance checks, resilience tests, error handling review.
acceptance_criteria: Startup and navigation are acceptable on target smartphones; large recipe lists remain usable; persistence failures show safe errors; unavailable/offline states match the data strategy.
tests: Framework performance/profile checks where available; unit/component tests for error paths; manual review if profiling is unavailable.
closes_when: Major MVP performance and resilience risks are tested or documented with follow-up gaps.
escalate_if: Performance work requires a real device fleet or production telemetry.
do_not: Do not add analytics/tracking unless a resolved decision explicitly requires it.

## GAP-034 — Prepare release readiness documentation
phase: 5
status: closed
type: release
blocked_by: GAP-032, GAP-033
goal: Document how to build, test, configure, and review privacy for an MVP release without committing secrets.
scope: README.md, docs/release.md, docs/privacy.md, app metadata placeholders if supported.
acceptance_criteria: Build/test commands are documented; signing and store-account steps are documented without secrets; privacy/data safety checklist reflects actual MVP data; placeholder app icon/splash requirements are tracked.
tests: Manual docs review; run documented local checks.
closes_when: A developer can prepare a release build path without guessing secrets, accounts, or privacy posture.
escalate_if: Signing keys, app-store accounts, legal policy approval, or production credentials are required.
do_not: Do not commit signing files, certificates, secrets, or real user data.

## GAP-035 — Codify implementation feedback direction
phase: 0
status: closed
type: product
blocked_by: none
goal: Convert implementation feedback into the next local-first kitchen assistant scope without expanding into social or marketplace features.
scope: PROJECT.md, docs/implementation-feedback-plan.md, docs/current-implementation-summary.md if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Feedback priorities are summarized as near-term build order; public publishing/social/marketplace remain out of scope; chef, dietician, and architecture recommendations are mapped to future gaps.
tests: Manual docs review; rg check that no new public publishing/marketplace work is made in-scope for the next implementation slice.
closes_when: The feedback plan gives a clear next-build direction and preserves current MVP boundaries.
escalate_if: Feedback requires clinical diet advice, public publishing, backend sync, AI integrations, or paid services as immediate scope.
do_not: Do not implement code or change architecture in this gap.

## GAP-036 — Upgrade recipe form dynamic collections
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-035, GAP-022
goal: Let users capture real recipes with multiple ingredient rows, grouped components, prep notes, multiple steps, visible notes, and time fields.
scope: src/features/recipes/presentation/RecipeFormScreen.tsx, recipe presentation tests, app integration tests, styles if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Users can add, duplicate, remove, and keyboard-reorder multiple ingredients and steps; ingredient rows include name, quantity, unit, group, and prep note; prep/cook time and notes are visible; submitting at least 10 ingredients and 6 steps reaches recipe use cases with arrays.
tests: Component tests for add/remove/duplicate/reorder ingredients and steps; integration test proving a multi-row recipe persists and renders after save.
closes_when: A normal multi-ingredient, multi-step recipe can be created and edited through tested UI without changing persistence by hand.
escalate_if: This requires Recipe v2 schema fields that current Recipe domain cannot safely represent.
do_not: Do not add nutrition, shopping lists, cook mode, or schema migration in this gap.

## GAP-037 — Connect recipe form to cookbook category picker
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-036, GAP-024
goal: Replace free-text category entry with a selectable category path from existing cookbook data.
scope: Recipe form presentation, AppRoutes dependency wiring, cookbook category helper tests.
acceptance_criteria: Recipe form loads cookbook categories, users select an existing category, saved recipe categoryPath matches selected category tree, and unavailable cookbook states are visible.
tests: Component tests for category loading, selection, empty categories, and save payload.
closes_when: New recipes no longer depend on manually typed category paths in the UI.
escalate_if: A permanent categoryId schema migration is required before the picker can be represented safely.
do_not: Do not implement drag-and-drop category ordering or public cookbook sharing.

## GAP-038 — Add grouped recipe detail and export v2
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-036, GAP-029
goal: Make recipe detail and private export useful to real cooks by preserving ingredient groups, prep notes, times, notes, and scaled/base quantities.
scope: Recipe detail presentation, recipe export use cases, export tests.
acceptance_criteria: Detail groups ingredients by component when present; export includes yield/servings, times, grouped ingredients, prep notes, steps, notes, and private-export disclaimer.
tests: Unit tests for export formatting; component tests for grouped detail display and export failure/success states.
closes_when: A shared text export is usable to cook from without opening the app.
escalate_if: Export requires public links, backend hosting, copyright moderation, or private credentials.
do_not: Do not add public publishing, social feed, or marketplace behavior.

## GAP-039 — Add kitchen-aware scaling modes
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-038
goal: Extend portion scaling with ingredient-level scale behavior for linear, integer, fixed, to-taste, and pan-dependent ingredients.
scope: Recipe domain, portion scaling domain, unit/formatting helpers, tests, docs/portion-scaling.md.
acceptance_criteria: Scale modes are explicit; integer items round to whole usable units; fixed/to-taste ingredients show safe guidance; pan-dependent items surface warnings; existing linear scaling remains backward compatible.
tests: Unit tests for every scale mode, quantity formatting, invalid inputs, and unchanged base recipe values.
closes_when: Portion previews can communicate when scaling is exact, rounded, fixed, to taste, or needs pan/cook-time review.
escalate_if: Full unit conversion, nutrition, or professional recipe formula support is required immediately.
do_not: Do not add nutrition calculations or shopping list behavior.

## GAP-040 — Add cook mode foundation
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-038, GAP-039
goal: Add a guided cook mode with large step cards, progress, checklist behavior, and persisted current step.
scope: Recipe presentation, cook session application state, local persistence adapter if needed, component/integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: Users can enter cook mode from detail, move forward/back through steps, mark steps complete, leave and return without losing progress, and see large mobile-readable content.
tests: Component tests for step navigation/progress/resume and integration test for persisted cook session.
closes_when: A user can cook from guided steps without relying on the dense detail page.
escalate_if: Timers, notifications, or platform wake-lock permissions become mandatory for the first cook mode slice.
do_not: Do not implement push notifications, camera, AI, or public sharing.

## GAP-041 — Add prep-ahead, storage, and leftovers guidance
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-036
goal: Model practical cooking guidance for prep-ahead, storage, reheating, holding, and leftover reuse.
scope: Recipe domain, recipe form/detail UI, export formatting, tests.
acceptance_criteria: Recipes can store and display prep-ahead, refrigerator/freezer, reheating, holding, and leftover reuse notes without medical or safety overclaims.
tests: Domain tests, form/detail component tests, export formatting tests.
closes_when: Planning-relevant storage and leftover guidance survives create/edit/detail/export.
escalate_if: The app needs regulated food-safety advice beyond simple user-entered notes and conservative reminders.
do_not: Do not provide automatic medical or food-safety guarantees.

## GAP-042 — Add flexible planner board and move-entry UI
phase: 3
status: closed
type: ui
blocked_by: GAP-025, GAP-041
goal: Expand planning beyond loop templates with configurable board presets, slots, move-entry controls, and planned cook/eat/prep context.
scope: Planner domain/application/presentation, planner tests.
acceptance_criteria: Users can configure weekly, rolling 7, month-list, or custom-loop board presets; optionally set a local start date; assign saved recipes to template/custom/no slots with cook/eat/prep context; move entries between days/slots; keep loop templates as reusable mode; and see empty/error states.
tests: Domain/use-case tests for board presets, slots, moves, invalid data, and legacy normalization; component tests for board setup, add/move/remove, and template mode; integration test from recipe to planner board to persisted reload.
closes_when: A user can plan what to cook, eat, or prep on a flexible board while retaining training/non-training loop templates.
escalate_if: Calendar integration, notifications, or sync are required.
do_not: Do not call platform calendar APIs or add push reminders.

## GAP-043 — Generate shopping list from meal plan
phase: 4
status: open
type: application
blocked_by: GAP-038, GAP-042
goal: Turn planned recipes into a grocery list with combined ingredients and source recipe references.
scope: src/features/shopping, planner application integration, shopping UI, tests.
acceptance_criteria: A shopping list can be generated from selected plan entries, duplicate ingredients combine when unit/name match, source recipes remain visible, items can be checked off, manual items can be added, and text export works.
tests: Domain/use-case tests for grouping and source refs; component tests for check-off/manual items; integration test from meal plan to shopping list.
closes_when: A planned week can become a usable grocery list without external services.
escalate_if: Barcode scanning, store inventory, pricing APIs, or account sync are required.
do_not: Do not add payments, store integrations, or tracking.

## GAP-044 — Add allergen and dietary awareness baseline
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-036
goal: Add conservative allergen and dietary metadata with verification status before any nutrition scoring.
scope: Nutrition/allergen domain or recipe metadata, recipe form/detail warnings, tests, docs/privacy.md if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Big 9 allergen flags and dietary tags can be recorded; every warning has unverified/estimated/userVerified status; UI never claims guaranteed safety without user verification.
tests: Domain tests for flags/status; component tests for warnings and wording.
closes_when: Users can see conservative allergen/dietary warnings on recipes without medical claims.
escalate_if: Clinical diet advice, diagnoses, or regulated health claims are required.
do_not: Do not calculate macros, prescribe diets, or guarantee allergen-free safety.

## GAP-045 — Add nutrition estimate MVP
phase: 2
status: closed
type: domain
blocked_by: GAP-044
goal: Add manual nutrition estimates with source/status labels for recipe and per-serving summaries.
scope: Nutrition domain/application, recipe detail/planner summaries, tests, docs/privacy.md.
acceptance_criteria: Users can enter calories/protein/carbs/fat/fiber/sodium as estimates; summaries are per recipe and per serving; every value carries notCalculated/estimated/partiallyMapped/userVerified status and source label.
tests: Domain/use-case tests for per-serving math and status; component tests for estimate labels.
closes_when: Nutrition data is useful but visibly estimated and non-clinical.
escalate_if: FoodData Central API, medical targets, or automatic diet recommendations are required.
do_not: Do not call external nutrition services or provide medical advice.

## GAP-046 — Migrate persistence to IndexedDB with schema v2
phase: 4
status: closed
type: data
blocked_by: GAP-041, GAP-044
goal: Move rich local-first data from localStorage JSON to IndexedDB with a tested v1-to-v2 migration path.
scope: Local database abstraction, data adapters, migration fixtures, backup docs, tests.
acceptance_criteria: Existing v1 localStorage data migrates without deletion; schema version 2 is written; recipes/cookbooks/plans persist in IndexedDB; corrupt records fail safely; localStorage is retained for small settings/migration marker only.
tests: Data/integration tests for migration, restart persistence, corrupt fixtures, and non-destructive fallback.
closes_when: Rich records can persist reliably and v1 users keep their data.
escalate_if: Browser support, encryption, multi-device sync, or external backup services become mandatory.
do_not: Do not add backend sync, auth, or real user data fixtures.

## GAP-047 — Add accessibility automation
phase: 5
status: open
type: quality
blocked_by: GAP-036
goal: Add automated accessibility checks for dynamic recipe forms and future cook mode before mobile conversion.
scope: Test tooling, component accessibility tests, docs/accessibility.md.
acceptance_criteria: Automated accessibility checks cover core screens; dynamic row controls are keyboard accessible; reorder actions have non-drag controls; errors are connected to fields; focus states remain visible.
tests: Accessibility test command plus component tests for dynamic row keyboard behavior.
closes_when: Accessibility regressions in core flows are caught locally before manual QA.
escalate_if: Tooling requires unsupported browser/device APIs or paid services.
do_not: Do not redesign product scope or add tracking.
